events.title: Mobile Legends Bang Bang: Bigetron by Vitality vs Team Liquid ID (BO3) - MPL Indonesia Regular Season
events.description:
This market refers to the Mobile Legends Bang Bang match between Bigetron by Vitality and Team Liquid ID in the MPL Indonesia Regular Season, initially scheduled for May 8 at 7:15AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Bigetron by Vitality" if Bigetron by Vitality win the match against Team Liquid ID.

This market will resolve to "Team Liquid ID" if Team Liquid ID win the match against Bigetron by Vitality.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://liquipedia.net/mobilelegends/Main_Page. However, if https://liquipedia.net/mobilelegends/Main_Page has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: Bigetron by Vitality sits third in MPL Indonesia Season 17 regular season standings at 6-4 (13-13 games), one match win ahead of fourth-place Team Liquid ID (6-5, 13-14), fueling trader focus on their Week 7 BO3 rematch scheduled for May 8. BTR's trader consensus edge stems from a 2-1 head-to-head win over TLID on April 18, extended by recent sweeps including 2-0 over RRQ Hoshi on May 2 and EVOS earlier in April, showcasing strong form amid patch 2.1.61 meta. TLID showed resilience with a 2-1 upset over NAVI on May 3 but faltered against top squads like ONIC and Dewa United. Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects tight uncertainty around Tel Aviv's May 9 highest temperature, with 27°C slightly leading at 23% implied probability amid 25–29°C outcomes dominating over 90% volume, driven by the latest Israel Meteorological Service (IMS) forecasts and ECMWF/GFS model ensembles projecting peaks in this range under a stable high-pressure ridge over the eastern Mediterranean. Following cooler early-May highs (21–22°C on May 5–6), subsidence and clear skies are fostering a warming trend, though coastal sea breezes may cap intensities near 25–26°C per cooler ECMWF runs, while GFS suggests potential for 28–29°C on sunnier scenarios. Diurnal variations and minor onshore flow discrepancies account for the clustered odds; monitor IMS evening updates and 00Z model refreshes for shifts before resolution at the official Tel Aviv station.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket has locked in a 100% implied probability for a highest temperature of 29°C in Kuala Lumpur on May 8, driven by real-time observations from the Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) at Kuala Lumpur International Airport (WMKK) station, where hourly readings peaked at 29°C amid persistent cloud cover and isolated thunderstorms. Convective activity during the inter-monsoon season suppressed solar insolation, preventing the typical May highs of 32–33°C seen in recent days like May 7's 34–35°C forecast. METAR reports and model consensus confirm no further warming potential post-afternoon, with sunset around 7 PM. Realistic challenges include rare data revisions from Wunderground's historical archive, though preliminary station data remains stable. Final MetMalaysia summary expected soon for resolution.

markets.question: Will AZ win on 2026-05-10?

events.title: Feyenoord Rotterdam vs. AZ
events.description: This event is for the upcoming Eredivisie game, scheduled for Sunday, May 10, 2026 between Feyenoord Rotterdam and AZ.

eventMetadata.context_description: Feyenoord's strong home record at De Kuip and second-place Eredivisie standing fuel trader consensus at 54% implied probability, despite a mounting injury list headlined by Jakub Moder's season-ending absence since late April, alongside doubts for Anel Ahmedhodzic, Leo Sauer, and In-beom Hwang. AZ Alkmaar, sitting sixth, enters as a 29% underdog after a resilient 3-3 draw in September's head-to-head, bolstered by fewer injury concerns but challenged by Feyenoord's unbeaten streak in the last eight meetings (seven wins, one draw). The 28% draw pricing underscores the closely contested nature, with both sides prioritizing clean sheets amid late-season table jostling.

markets.question: Strait of Hormuz traffic returns to normal by end of May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if IMF Portwatch publishes a 7-day moving average of transit calls (“Arrivals of Ships”) for the Strait of Hormuz equal to or above 60 for any date between market creation and May 31, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Daily transit calls include container, dry bulk, roll-on/roll-off, general cargo, and tanker ships. Ships not reported by IMF Portwatch will not be considered.

This market will resolve as soon as IMF Portwatch publishes a 7-day moving average of transit calls equal to or above the specified level, or once data has been published for the final date in the specified period and no such value has been published. If no data has been published for the final date of the specified period within 14 calendar days (ET) after the end of that period, this market will resolve based on data published up to that point.

Revisions to previously published data points made within this market’s timeframe will be considered. However, they will not disqualify a previously published data point from qualifying. Revisions to previously published data points after data is published for May 31, 2026, however, will not be considered.

The resolution source for this market will be IMF Portwatch, specifically the transit calls data published for the Strait of Hormuz at https://portwatch.imf.org/pages/cb5856222a5b4105adc6ee7e880a1730, both in the chart and through downloadable files.

eventMetadata.context_description: Ongoing Iranian interference and recent military clashes in the Strait of Hormuz, including U.S. destroyers repelling fast-attack boats just days ago and Iranian missile strikes on naval vessels on May 4, keep commercial shipping traffic at a trickle—typically 5-10 vessels daily versus pre-conflict averages over 100. Despite a fragile April 8 ceasefire and U.S. "Project Freedom" efforts to escort ships, no comprehensive deal has emerged to halt attacks, clear sea mines (which U.S. officials estimate could take six months), or lift sanctions, while Gulf states back a U.S.-led UN resolution demanding free passage. Traders' 72.5% "No" consensus on normalization by May's end reflects persistent security risks, elevated insurance premiums, and stranded vessels in the Persian Gulf amid the unresolved U.S.-Iran deadlock.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket tilts modestly toward a Houston high of 80-81°F (27% implied probability) on May 9, with 82-83°F (21%) and 78-79°F (16.5%) close behind, capturing model ensemble spread amid inherent 2-3 day forecast uncertainty. National Weather Service guidance and major models (GFS, ECMWF, Canadian) converge on afternoon highs near 81°F under partly cloudy conditions, moderated by Gulf of Mexico moisture fueling sea breeze circulation and potential cumulus cloud development that caps peak heating. Differentiating factors include divergent simulations of diurnal convection timing, boundary layer winds (northerly shear vs. southerly flow), and residual soil moisture from prior showers limiting sensible heating below climatological May norms of ~86°F. Watch 00Z model runs overnight and NWS updates tomorrow for refinements ahead of resolution.

markets.question: Will Arvid Lindblad be the 2026 F1 Drivers' Champion?
markets.description:

This market will resolve as soon as the official results of the final scheduled race of the 2026 F1 season are known.

If multiple drivers tie for first place in the drivers standings, this market will resolve according to the tiebreak procedure used by F1 to determine the 2026 F1 Drivers’ champion.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed driver to win the 2026 F1 Drivers Championship based on the rules of F1 (e.g., they are mathematically eliminated from contention), the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the F1 season is permanently canceled or has not been completed by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Formula 1.

markets.question: Will May 2026 be the 2nd hottest on record?
markets.description:

Note: If May 2026 is tied for first, second, or third hottest with another year, it will qualify for the bracket it ties with.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the figures found in the table titled "GLOBAL Land-Ocean Temperature Index in 0.01 degrees Celsius" under the column "May" (https://data.giss.nasa.gov/gistemp/tabledata_v4/GLB.Ts+dSST.txt). If NASA's "Global Temperature Index" is rendered permanently unavailable, other information from NASA may be used.
If no information for May 2026 is provided by NASA by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, a consensus of credible sources will be used to resolve this market.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the NY-16 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the NY-16 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

While probabilities exceed 90%, an upset remains possible via a major Latimer scandal, health issue, legal challenge, or a national Republican midterm wave boosting turnout in urban suburbs, though such shifts are rare in safe seats ahead of the November 3 general election.

markets.question: Will Google have the #3 AI model at the end of May 2026 (Style Control On)?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the company that owns the model that has the third-highest arena rank (Style Control On) based on the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard (https://lmarena.ai/) when the table under the "Leaderboard" tab is checked on May 31, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

Results from the "Rank" column under the "Text Arena | Overall" Leaderboard tab at https://lmarena.ai/leaderboard/text with style control on will be used to resolve this market.

Models will be ordered primarily by their leaderboard rank at the market’s check time. If two or more models are tied on rank, they will be ordered by their Arena score, including any underlying, unrounded, granular values reflected in the data below the leaderboard. If a tie remains, alphabetical order of company names as listed in this market group will be used as a final tiebreaker (e.g., if the two models are tied by exact arena score, “Google” would be ranked ahead of “xAI”). This market will resolve based on the company that occupies third place under this ranking system.

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source is unavailable at check time, this market will remain open until the leaderboard comes back online and will resolve based on the first check after it becomes available. If it becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve based on another resolution source.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Democrat Janelle Bynum's bid for reelection in Oregon's 5th Congressional District has solidified trader consensus at 82.5% for the Democratic Party, bolstered by her strong fundraising, endorsements from EMILYs List and pro-Israel groups, and a district that delivered solid support for Kamala Harris in 2024. Recent Cook Political Report upgrades to Likely Democrat reflect Republicans' recruitment struggles, with Deschutes County Commissioner Patti Adair and challenger Jonathan Lockwood as the leading GOP primary contenders lacking broader appeal in this former battleground. The May 19 primary looms as the next catalyst, but historical incumbency advantages and weak opposition sustain high Democratic implied probabilities amid limited polling.

This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Korea Republic vs. Czechia
events.description: This event is for the upcoming FIFA World Cup game, scheduled for Thursday, June 11, 2026 between Korea Republic and Czechia.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices Korea Republic a slim 34.5% favorite over Czechia at 34.0% with draw at 31.0%, capturing the razor-thin dynamics of this FIFA World Cup Group A opener at neutral Estadio Akron in high-altitude Guadalajara. Czechia's momentum from their March playoff triumph over Denmark via penalties contrasts South Korea's back-to-back friendly losses to Ivory Coast and Austria, fostering uncertainty despite Korea's higher 25th FIFA ranking versus Czechia's 41st. Limited head-to-head history leans toward draws, while potential absences like South Korea's Hwang In-beom and Jae-Sung Lee alongside Czechia's Ladislav Krejcí add volatility to lineups heavy on stars like Son Heung-min and Tomas Soucek. Both sides eye crucial early points against a Mexico-led group.

markets.question: Will Partido dos Trabalhadores (PT) win the most seats in the next Brazilian Senate election?
markets.description:
The next federal Senate election is scheduled to take place in Brazil on October 4, 2026, in which two-thirds of the Senate’s 81 seats will be contested.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the greatest number of seats in the next Brazilian Senate election. 

Only seats contested in the next Brazilian Senate election will be considered.

If the result of this election isn't known definitively by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Brazilian government, specifically the Superior Electoral Court (Tribunal Superior Eleitoral, TSE) (e.g., www.tse.jus.br/eleicoes/resultados-eleicoes).

events.title: Next Brazil Senate Election: Most Seats Won
events.description:
The next federal Senate election is scheduled to take place in Brazil on October 4, 2026, in which two-thirds of the Senate’s 81 seats will be contested.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the greatest number of seats in the next Brazilian Senate election. 

Only seats contested in the next Brazilian Senate election will be considered.

In the event of a tie between multiple parties for the most seats won, this market will resolve in favor of the party whose listed abbreviation appears first in alphabetical order.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party in the Brazilian Senate, not by any federation or coalition it may be a part of.

If the result of this election isn't known definitively by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors the PL party securing the most seats among the 27 up for grabs in Brazil's October 4, 2026, Senate election—one per state and the Federal District—reflecting recent state-level polls from institutes like Quaest, Genial/Quaest, and Paraná Pesquisas in late April showing PL candidates leading or competitive in key battlegrounds such as Santa Catarina (Carol de Toni and Carlos Bolsonaro topping Neokemp), Paraná (Filipe Barros strong), and São Paulo (Guilherme Derrite close). This momentum stems from the Bolsonaro family's enduring appeal, boosted by Flávio Bolsonaro's presidential polling surge tying President Lula in runoffs, alongside Senate defeats of government bills underscoring opposition strength. PSB trails at 14% buoyed by Simone Tebet's frontrunner status in São Paulo post-filiation, while UNIÃO lags amid fragmented centrist fields; late scandals or shifts could alter dynamics before the vote.
